title: JavaScript Image Maps Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of map and area elements and interactive event handling in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the purpose of an image map?
options: To group multiple images into one.
To create clickable areas (hotspots) on a single image.
To make an image load faster.
To provide a caption for an image.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the correct syntax for an image that uses an image map?
Which of the following is NOT a standard shape for an <area> tag in an image map?
Can you use addEventListener() on an <area> tag within an image map?
options: Yes, like any other HTML element.
No, area elements cannot trigger JavaScript events.
Only if you add the event listener to the tag instead.
Only in modern SVG-based image maps.

questions.question: How can you specify that a hotpot in an image map should be a circle at coordinates 90, 58 with radius 3?
